source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, $BC=\sqrt{6}+\sqrt{2}$, $\angle C=45^\circ$, $AB=\sqrt{2}AC$, what is the length of $AC$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Draw line AD perpendicular to BC through point A, with D being the foot of the perpendicular, as shown in the diagram.\\
Let AC = x, then AB = $\sqrt{2}x$.\\
In $\triangle ACD$, AD = AC$\cdot\sin C = \frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}x$,\\
CD = AC$\cdot\cos C = \frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}x$;\\
In $\triangle ABD$, AB = $\sqrt{2}x$, AD = $\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}x$,\\
$\therefore$BD = $\sqrt{AB^{2}-AD^{2}} = \frac{\sqrt{6}}{2}x$,\\
$\therefore$BC = BD + CD = $\frac{\sqrt{6}}{2}x + \frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}x = \sqrt{6} + \sqrt{2}$,\\
$\therefore x = \boxed{2}$.
